reforms.description: The French government has implemented several initiatives to support the startup ecosystem, including the ACCRE programme, which provides financial assistance and social security exemptions for unemployed individuals starting their own businesses. Additionally, guarantees for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) have been substantially increased to facilitate access to credit, and the creation of the New Market in February 1996 aims to improve financing for innovative enterprises by enhancing the liquidity of investments in rapidly expanding SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to enhance access to financing and support for new enterprises, which are critical for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been actively in place since their introduction in 1996.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms significantly support the startup ecosystem, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The ACCRE programme provides financial help to the unemployed who want to become self-employed in addition to an exemption from social security contributions for one year.

reforms.description: The French government's privatisation programme aimed to enhance market efficiency and corporate governance by selling off state-owned enterprises operating in competitive sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing market efficiency and corporate governance, the privatisation programme is expected to foster a more competitive environment conducive to innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully executed since its initiation in 1993.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the economy, impacting the overall innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Resuming the privatisation plan was one of the priorities of the incoming administration, who in mid-1993 presented a list of firms to be sold and issued a framework law.
